verdict_reasoning: ITPR3 (inositol 1,4,5-trisphosphate receptor type 3) is an ER membrane-resident tetrameric Ca2+ release channel. Its bulk N-terminal IP3-binding and regulatory domains face the cytoplasm; the six TM segments and pore are embedded in the ER membrane. Not cell_state_induced: no documented stress/activation-driven PM display of the protein body. Not tissue_restricted_surface: ER localization is universal. Not lysosomal_exocytosis: ER, not lysosomal. Not dual_localization: minor reports of PM-associated IP3R activity exist but ITPR3 is overwhelmingly ER-resident with no validated surface pool accessible to extracellular binders. Not stable_surface_attachment: it is a polytopic TM protein, not a secreted ligand.
